response.justification: Strong distributed-systems background (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP req/s) and current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq align well with the role's mindset and architectural demands. However, Python — a must-have — is conspicuously absent from the skills list and employment descriptions; the stack history is heavily TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Staff-level technical strategy and stakeholder coordination experience is implied but not strongly evidenced. Worth a screen to verify Python depth and scope of technical leadership.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience (Sociomantic, millions req/s)
Current hands-on AI-agent infra and MCP tooling work at re:cinq
Deep TypeScript/Node.js plus strong craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
concerns: Python not listed in skills or job descriptions despite being a must-have
Limited explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management at staff scale
No mention of on-call/incident-response ownership in prior roles
